steps.title: Follow the process order
steps.content: The first sentence describes the first stage of Australia's Senate proportional representation process: citizens cast votes for political parties rather than individual candidates. The next sentence describes what happens after votes are counted: parties receive seats in proportion to their votes.

steps.title: Choose a sequence transition
steps.content: Choice A and Choice C signal contrast, but the sentences describe stages of the same process. Choice B signals restatement, though the second sentence adds the next step. A temporal transition showing what happens after voting and counting is needed. The answer is D.
